title: Search is really slow
body: The search feature used to be fast but now it takes forever. Sometimes it takes 10-15 seconds to get results. I have a lot of tasks but this seems excessive. Running TaskFlow on my work laptop.

comments.author: triage-agent
comments.body:
Thanks for reporting this! I'd like to understand the change you noticed. You mentioned search used to be fast — can you recall roughly when it started slowing down, and whether anything changed around that time? For example, did you upgrade TaskFlow, import a batch of tasks, or notice the slowdown after your task count grew past a certain point?

Also, when you say you have 'a lot of tasks,' could you give me a rough sense of the number? Hundreds, thousands, more?

comments.author: reporter
comments.body: I think it started maybe 2 weeks ago? I did upgrade to v2.3 around that time, so that could be related. I have around 5,000 tasks — I've been using TaskFlow for about 2 years now. It was always snappy before, even with all those tasks.

comments.author: triage-agent
comments.body:
That's really helpful — the v2.3 upgrade timing is a strong clue. I'd like to understand the slowdown a bit more concretely.

When you run a search, can you walk me through what you experience? For instance, does the UI freeze entirely for those 10-15 seconds, or do you see a spinner/progress indicator? And is every search slow, or does it depend on what you're searching for — like, is searching for a single word just as slow as a more complex query? I'm curious whether the delay feels like it's happening at the start (before anything happens) or at the end (like it's processing for a long time).

comments.author: reporter
comments.body: No spinner or anything, the UI just kind of hangs for a while and then the results pop up. It feels like it's processing the whole time. Now that you mention it, I think searching by task title is still pretty fast actually — it's when I search across the task descriptions that it really bogs down. I have some tasks with really long descriptions (like pasted meeting notes and stuff) so maybe that's part of it? But it used to handle those fine before.

comments.author: triage-agent
comments.body: [RESOLVED] {"title":"Search regression in v2.3: full-text search across task descriptions extremely slow (~10-15s) with large task count","problem":"After upgrading to v2.3, searching across task descriptions takes 10-15 seconds and blocks the UI. Title-only search remains fast. The reporter has ~5,000 tasks accumulated over 2 years, some with very long descriptions (pasted meeting notes). Search was consistently fast before the upgrade.","root_cause_hypothesis":"The v2.3 release likely introduced a change to how description search is performed — most probably a dropped or missing full-text index on the description field, a query change that bypasses an existing index, or a switch from indexed search to naive string scanning. The fact that title search is unaffected suggests the regression is isolated to the description search code path.","reproduction_steps":["Create or import a dataset with ~5,000 tasks, some with long descriptions (several paragraphs each)","Run TaskFlow v2.2 (or prior) and perform a search that matches against description content — confirm it is fast","Upgrade to v2.3","Perform the same description-targeted search and observe the ~10-15 second hang","Compare with a title-only search on v2.3 to confirm title search is still fast"],"environment":"TaskFlow v2.3, work laptop (OS unspecified), ~5,000 tasks with some containing long descriptions (pasted meeting notes)","severity":"high","impact":"Any user with a moderately large task database (~5,000+ tasks) who searches by description content will experience a multi-second UI freeze. This degrades a core workflow — search — from instant to unusable. Users with long-form descriptions are likely hit hardest.","recommended_fix":"Diff the search implementation between v2.2 and v2.3, focusing on the description search query path. Check for: (1) dropped or missing full-text index on the description column, (2) query changes that cause a full table scan instead of using an index, (3) any new processing (e.g., snippet extraction, highlighting) that iterates over full description text in-memory. Also investigate why the search blocks the UI thread — the search should be async regardless of backend performance.","proposed_test_case":"Performance test: with a dataset of 5,000 tasks (including 500+ with descriptions over 1,000 characters), a description search query should return results in under 2 seconds. Additionally, verify the UI remains responsive (shows a loading indicator, does not freeze) during any search operation.","information_gaps":["Exact OS and platform (desktop app vs. web) — unlikely to affect the core search regression but may matter for UI thread blocking behavior","Whether the reporter's database was migrated as part of the v2.3 upgrade (migration could have dropped an index)","Exact prior version (v2.2 assumed but not confirmed)"]}
